The revolt took four days https://thenextweb.com/news/hubspot-reverses-customer-data-ai-feature-opt-out Wed, 08 Jul 2026 20:28:58 +0000 TheNextWeb=7128e94c510a1345548fc4e5568f01c9 HubSpot has scrapped a plan to use its customers’ data for a new AI feature, just four days after announcing it. The CRM firm changed its terms on 1 July to pool customer data, including contact and employer details, for a tool that finds sales leads, The Information reported. It opted users in by default. Samsung Heavy Industries plans to launch its first floating data centre by 2028, Seoul Economic Daily reported. It would be a purpose-built barge parked near the coast. The design is specific. Rather than convert an old ship, […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Nvidia teams up with chip rival d-Matrix instead of fighting it https://thenextweb.com/news/nvidia-d-matrix-joint-inference-chip-system Wed, 08 Jul 2026 20:07:22 +0000 TheNextWeb=429cba4dfdb3f5f59c3acdda7d097f6a Nvidia has found a new way to handle its chip rivals: work with them. The GPU giant is combining its hardware with inference chips from the startup d-Matrix, The Information reported. The two will ship a joint system to run AI models. The AI-cloud firm Parasail will be its first customer, with the system due […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> A £35bn plan to fill the UK with 14 mini nuclear reactors https://thenextweb.com/news/solowow-sge-35bn-uk-small-modular-reactors-bwrx-300 Wed, 08 Jul 2026 20:01:02 +0000 TheNextWeb=55b03037ab751630c8659ebd14576103 A Polish billionaire has laid out plans to build a fleet of small nuclear reactors across Britain, at an estimated £35bn, TechRadar reported. Michał Sołowow’s firm, SGE, says it wants to install 14 GE Vernova Hitachi BWRX-300 reactors on three UK sites. It is targeting first power in 2034. The numbers are large. Together the […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Venus Aerospace raises $91M to build its ‘detonation’ rocket engine https://thenextweb.com/news/venus-aerospace-91m-series-b-rotating-detonation-rocket-engine Wed, 08 Jul 2026 19:50:37 +0000 TheNextWeb=8a64f8f12db123dbad0c8bf6800742d2 A Texas startup has raised $91m to build a rocket engine most of the industry once wrote off as too hard. Venus Aerospace announced a $91m Series B to develop its Rotating Detonation Rocket Engine, or RDRE. Mercury Fund led the round. The backers read like a defence roll-call. Lockheed Martin Ventures joined, alongside MESH, […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> A tritium ‘nuclear battery’ just reached orbit for the first time https://thenextweb.com/news/city-labs-bohr-first-commercial-nuclear-power-space Wed, 08 Jul 2026 19:29:08 +0000 TheNextWeb=97d19ec1299d17fc7b63b0eb80ab9b1e The nuclear age has quietly reached commercial spaceflight. City Labs, a Miami firm, has launched BOHR, which it calls the world’s first commercial nuclear-powered satellite and the first nuclear CubeSat. The craft is about the size of a softball. It flew on a SpaceX Falcon 9 rideshare on 7 July, alongside 80 other payloads, the […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Smart glasses are having a privacy reckoning, and Meta is caught in the middle https://thenextweb.com/news/smart-glasses-privacy-new-york-courthouse-ban-meta-recording-light Wed, 08 Jul 2026 19:05:56 +0000 TheNextWeb=74e6418b6a200502dc02d55fdf1a00b7 The smart-glasses backlash has reached the courtroom. From 20 July, New York will ban recording eyewear from every one of its 1,240 courts. It is the first US state to go that far. An internal memo from the New York State Unified Court System sets out the rule, first reported by Syracuse.com. The ban is […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Musk’s SpaceXAI ships ‘Opus-class’ Grok 4.5 to undercut OpenAI and Anthropic https://thenextweb.com/news/spacexai-grok-4-5-cursor-opus-class-coding-model Wed, 08 Jul 2026 18:42:22 +0000 TheNextWeb=2680d306830f7fe3bf8cf37f4d128a19 SpaceXAI has launched Grok 4.5, its most capable model yet. It is the company’s first release since going public and buying the AI coding startup Cursor. This is the joint model the two firms had raced to ship. Elon Musk aimed it squarely at coding and agentic work, not casual chat. “It is an Opus-class […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Meta’s AI campus flushed a rare bacterium into Cheyenne’s water. The city hit back https://thenextweb.com/news/meta-data-centre-contractor-wyoming-water-contamination-cheyenne Wed, 08 Jul 2026 18:30:50 +0000 TheNextWeb=c52fb07e0418dd2e86b91262ddc4d5e0 A Meta contractor flushed a rare, potentially deadly bacterium into Cheyenne’s wastewater system. The Wyoming city has now suspended all data centre discharge, a fresh flashpoint in the fight over AI’s thirst for water. Officials in Cheyenne, Wyoming, have stopped accepting industrial wastewater from data centres. The trigger was a contractor building Meta’s new AI […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> This AI startup pays $1.7M a year in rent so staff will work 72-hour weeks https://thenextweb.com/news/rilla-housing-stipend-72-hour-week-ai-startup Wed, 08 Jul 2026 18:08:28 +0000 TheNextWeb=93b83abdeef086ecdc99cd34c398d1b1 Tech loves a perk, but few go this far. Rilla, an AI startup that makes coaching software for sales teams, spends about $1.7m a year on housing stipends so staff can live near its New York office, Fortune reports. The trade-off is a 72-hour week. Employees who live within a 10-minute bike ride of the […] This story continues at The Next Web]]>
